agent_runs.outcome: submitted first try - ZERO validation errors (33 claims incl. generated IN/OUT, 27 deps, 5 bug candidates)
ACCEPT (60/60 coverage: 31 supported, 1 partially_supported, 1 not_supported at MEDIUM confidence, 27/27 deps confirmed)

notes: Cheapest config overall (~249k tokens), first-round ACCEPT, zero validation retries.

The opus judge produced the two sharpest findings of the whole benchmark: (1) CL-007's 'EVERY write is serialized' disproved by showing add() INSERTs without lock() - a real overstatement; (2) CL-026's line count off by one because the last line has no trailing newline (wc -l vs physical lines) - flagged at medium confidence as a definitional ambiguity, so it downgraded without hard-blocking.

not_supported at medium confidence does not trigger the S-count hard block (only high-confidence wrong counts do) - the gate calculus worked exactly as designed: the page ships with the claim downgraded rather than the batch churning.
The fable author cited the largest verified dependency set (27) including dynamic-dispatch dependencies, with zero rejected.
